mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate wage income and consumption demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day SPX returns are not positive following PAYEMS increases in more than 55% of monthly observations, or the Rank Information Coefficient falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 800 thousand units (HOUST > 800) signal a durable recovery in residential construction and downstream demand for building materials, appliances, and household credit, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months when HOUST > 800, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or not significantly positive relative to baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the positive hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is above 7% and payroll employment rises month-over-month, the combination of labor market slack and positive net job creation signals an early-cycle recovery that lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition (UNRATE > 7% and rising PAYEMS) fails to produce positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or an interaction term in a bivariate regression is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime with elevated unemployment and near-zero policy rates, month-over-month increases in consumer prices reflect successful reflation of aggregate demand rather than overheating, reducing real debt burdens and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with slack labor market
failure_condition: During the qualifying regime, positive month-over-month CPIAUCSL changes do not coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of months, or the mean forward return is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned below 0.25%, extremely accommodative monetary policy compresses short-term discount rates and the equity risk premium, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive during FEDFUNDS < 0.25% periods (t-test p > 0.05), or the positive hit rate falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with a declining unemployment rate jointly signal broad-based demand strength and labor market tightening, which together drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of rising RSAFS and falling UNRATE does not produce positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term in a two-factor regression is insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days because it signals weakening manufacturing activity and lower near-term corporate revenue.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in INDPRO fail to predict negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or the Rank IC is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest-rate environment (FEDFUNDS < 1%), month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days because cheap capital amplifies the earnings impact of operating leverage in manufacturing.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with manufacturing expansion
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS < 1%, rising INDPRO fails to produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of months, or the conditional mean return is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).
